reviews.review_msg: all he does is read his powerpoints. exams are nothing like what he gives for homework. he doesn't care about students and isnt too friendly
hes  a really good teacher as long as you read the book youll do great
Nice guy, and a pretty easy class.  Easy to ace assignments, midterm was kind of hard but final was easier.  The prof overall has a good attitude.
A student can get a great grade from this course by this  Prof as long as he/she is good at ECON302! I rarely learnt anything from this course.
He is willing to explain the graphs step by step and the final was extremely easy. recommended
Nice prof, hard TA. He sometimes repeat himself but he just want to make sure we understand the material. Fair midterm, harder final
extremely great prof in SFU,one of the best prof i have even seen in sfu.i highly recommand you to take his course.
Great! Ghosh is very nice and approachable. His lectures were clear and organized. Exams were very fair. Overall just great prof. Very recommended.
He's very nice

This prof is so much better than comments on here indicate. His exams are extremely easy (half of the questions are from practice midterms/ tutorial questions). He is extremely helpful and wants his students to do well. I expected a boring class and actually came to really enjoy it. Put in the work and you can reap HUGE rewards! Take his classes!
greattttttttttttt
couldn't understand him! went to all the lectures and tutorials and still had no idea what was going on! exams were alright...except the fact that the mc were weighed very heavily.
Very nice, easy prof. Midterm was okay, and final was easy.

First of all his Exams, Assignments and midterm all are difficult. not tht "i can do it difficult" but hard core difficult. firstly u cant understand wat he is teaching first few classes he dint know how to on the mic. If a prof asks a difficult question its ok but a prof shud teach before putting tht difficult question everywhere. Accent is OKAY.

I don't understand all of the negatives about him.  His accent is fine & you can understand him.  He is very helpful and very nice. Probably one of the most polite profs @ sfu. The textbook is very important and if you know it you should get a B easily. My ta was awesome 2. If u get an Asian speaking English you will get him 2. He speaks fine
Void!!!Unless you are good at catching accents.My TA was better at explaning than him

He is very hard to understand - accent with a soft of lisp - shouldn't be allowed to teach. The lecture slides are basically from the book, so if you have a hard time understanding accents don't go to class.  Doesn't understand when his mic isn't working.  Boring lectures. If you're doing ECON103, don't get him as a prof!

I thought he was going to be a horrible prof because of his accent and the whole ordeal with "I couldn't find the lecture room (it was at C9001)," but he's actually nice. Chill person to talk to during office hour and he will help you with the assignment more than the TA I had. My TA sucked hardcore.

you cant understand anything he says. the tutorials are not mandatory. if you study the textbook you will pass but you better know it all. hes lecture slides are good but he basically says exaclty what he wrote on the slides in his lectures. DONT TAKE THIS CLASS. really.
He interacts with the class quite often and is helpful during office hours.  Also, very approachable.

He is a really good prof. Always willing to help and actually cares about his students.  He explains really well in lectures.  If you attend lectures and keep up with the notes, you will definitely do well in his class. I highly recommend him.
First day of class, he was late and his accent really drove me nut!

Honestly, take Ghosh for an easy A. The practice midterm was basically the same as the midterm. Most of the questions were directly from the textbook or his notes. No need to attend class, just look through his powerpoints. He is terrible, but the exams are easy.
First day of class, 156 students, 3rd week of class 46... you decide...
